sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: ACTION7, RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 111, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Agent started at bottom (rows 36-41), moved to top (rows 12-17). Agent at cols 11-16, blocks at cols 42-45. Agent can't reach blocks directly.
- Plan: Try ACTION7 to retract trail, then RIGHT to see if agent can move horizontally when trail is retracted.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 110 actions; a strong score needs about 61 or fewer. You are 1.8x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After ACTION7 + RIGHT:
- Agent still at rows 12-17, cols 11-16 (didn't move)
- But RIGHT had gameplay_changed=True this time!
- Horizontal trail: c rows 12-13 cols 17-46
Wait, earlier RIGHT had gameplay_changed=False. But now RIGHT has gameplay_changed=True.
What's different? Before, I pressed RIGHT when the trail was already extended. Now I pressed ACTION7 first (retract trail), then RIGHT.
So RIGHT after ACTION7 changes gameplay, but RIGHT when the trail is already extended doesn't change gameplay.
This suggests that RIGHT extends the trail, and the trail's length IS part of the gameplay state (when it changes).
But earlier, RIGHT had gameplay_changed=False. This means the trail was already at max length, and pressing RIGHT again didn't change anything.
Wait, but the result said "gameplay_changed=False, board_changed=True" for RIGHT earlier. This means the board changed (maybe the trail extended slightly), but the gameplay state didn't change.
Hmm, this is confusing. Let me think about this differently.
Maybe the trail has a max length, and once it reaches max length, further RIGHT actions don't change gameplay.
Or maybe the trail's length is only part of the gameplay state when it overlaps a block.
Let me check the current trail length. The horizontal trail is at cols 17-46, which is 30 columns wide.
The red block is at cols 42-45. So the trail overlaps the red block.
Let me try pressing RIGHT again to see if the trail extends further.
Actually, let me first check what happened when I pressed ACTION7. The trail was retracted, and then RIGHT extended it again.
So the trail's length is determined by the number of RIGHT actions since the last ACTION7.
Let me try: ACTION7, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT... to extend the trail past the blocks.
But wait, the trail is already at cols 17-46, which overlaps the red block at cols 42-45.
If I press RIGHT again, the trail would extend to cols 17-49 or so.
Let me try pressing RIGHT a few more times to see if the trail extends past all three blocks.
Actually, the blocks are at cols 42-45, which is the right edge of the panel (cols 17-46). So the trail can't extend past the blocks because the panel ends at col 46.
Wait, but the trail is at cols 17-46, which is the full width of the panel. So the trail is already at max length.
Let me try pressing RIGHT again to see what happens.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: RIGHT after ACTION7 has gameplay_changed=True. Trail extends from agent to right edge of panel. Trail overlaps red block.
Plan: Try pressing RIGHT multiple times to see if trail extends further, or try DOWN to move agent to different block rows.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try RIGHT again to see if trail extends further
result = action(['RIGHT'])
print(f"RIGHT: gameplay_changed={result.get('gameplay_changed')}, board_changed={result.get('board_changed')}")

# Check trail position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('w', 'g', 'c') and n['pixels'] > 20:
        b = n['boundary']
        r_min, r_max = min(p[0] for p in b), max(p[0] for p in b)
        c_min, c_max = min(p[1] for p in b), max(p[1] for p in b)
        if r_max - r_min < 5 and c_max - c_min > 10:  # Horizontal
            print(f"Horizontal trail: {n['color']} rows {r_min}-{r_max} cols {c_min}-{c_max} pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Now try DOWN to move agent to red block's row
# Agent at rows 12-17, red block at rows 19-22
# Need to move DOWN 2 rows to align with red block
result2 = action(['DOWN', 'DOWN'])
print(f"\nDOWN,DOWN: gameplay_changed={result2.get('gameplay_changed')}, board_changed={result2.get('board_changed')}, level_completed={result2.get('level_completed')}")

# Check agent position
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 18:
        b = n['boundary']
        print(f"Agent: rows {min(p[0] for p in b)}-{max(p[0] for p in b)}, cols {min(p[1] for p in b)}-{max(p[1] for p in b)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
RIGHT: gameplay_changed=False, board_changed=False
Horizontal trail: c rows 12-13 cols 17-46 pixels=60
Horizontal trail: g rows 53-53 cols 0-34 pixels=35

DOWN,DOWN: gameplay_changed=True, board_changed=True, level_completed=False
Agent: rows 12-17, cols 11-16
Agent: rows 56-61, cols 20-25
